name: Divot Golf Club
about: Divot Golf Club is a member-only golf simulator lounge located in Sandy, Utah. The facility provides 24/7 access to its private simulator bays, focusing on utilizing Trackman technology for advanced swing and ball data analysis to help golfers improve their game. With a limited membership capped at 35 individuals, the venue offers a premium, focused environment for practice and play. The primary model is based on membership tiers, allowing unlimited access to the simulator for up to 3 hours per booking, complemented by access to over 270 virtual golf courses and extensive practice data.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to professional-grade Trackman 4 technology with 26 data points for precise swing and ball analysis
24/7 availability and year-round play, unaffected by weather or holidays
Access to over 270 world-renowned golf courses and continuous software updates

food_and_drink.notes: BYOB policy is not explicitly mentioned, but no on-site kitchen or food service is described.
No on-site bar or alcohol service is mentioned in the provided content.

simulator_hardware.notes: Ground-based launch monitor using two radars and one camera with Optically Enhanced Radar Tracking (OERT). Positioned 8 feet behind the golfer.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The provided content does not specify whether rental clubs are available. It is recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience with the Trackman technology.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not have a clear policy on outside food and drink. While not explicitly forbidden, the focus is on golf practice and simulation, not food service.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: An 18-hole round can be played on the simulator, but a specific time frame is not provided. The duration will depend on the course selected and the pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Divot Golf Club is a member-only lounge focused on adult golf practice. While the technology could be used by juniors, the described environment and 21+ age-restricted events indicate it is not marketed as a family-friendly venue.
